summary:
Loose dentures soon after insertion are primarily due to an improperly designed or poorly fitting prosthesis that fails to achieve adequate surface contact and retention initially. This rapid onset indicates a lack of secure bonding right from the beginning without gradual adjustment typical of new fittings. Agent1 concludes that poor design/fitting leading to immediate insecurity best explains early loose dentures among provided options based on consistent clinical observations and evidence-based understanding. 

The summary has been condensed into exactly 2 clear concise informative sentences focusing only on critical aspects directly related to answering the posed medical question regarding causes of quickly becoming loose dentures. No extraneous language was included. Key concepts like poor fit/design causing instant problems versus normal long-term adaptations were highlighted succinctly yet effectively conveying essential information needed to understand root cause being addressed by answer choice D ("improper fit").
